text.NRSV: The word of the Lord that came to Hosea son of Beeri, in the days of Kings Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah of Judah, and in the days of King Jeroboam son of Joash of Israel.
text.Rotherham: The word of Yahweh which came unto Hosea son of Beeri, in the days of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, Hezekiah, kings of Judah,—and in the days of Jeroboam son of Joash, king of Israel.
text.KJV: 1:1 The word of the Lord that came vnto Hosea, the sonne of Beeri, in the dayes of Uzziah, Iotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah kings of Iudah, and in the dayes of Ieroboam the sonne of Ioash king of Israel.
text.DRV: 1:1 The word of our Lord, that was made to Osee the son of Beeri, in the days of Ozias, Joathan, Achaz, Ezechias, kings of Juda, and in the days of Jeroboam the son of Joas king of Israel.

text.NRSV: When the Lord first spoke through Hosea, the Lord said to Hosea, “Go, take for yourself a wife of whoredom and have children of whoredom, for the land commits great whoredom by forsaking the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: The beginning of the word of Yahweh with Hosea, was,—that Yahweh said unto Hosea, Go take thee a woman of unchastity, and the children of unchastity, for, unchastely indeed, hath the land been going away from following Yahweh.
text.KJV: 1:2 The beginning of the word of the Lord by Hosea: and the Lord sayd to Hosea, Goe, take vnto thee a wife of whoredomes, and children of whoredomes: for the land hath committed great whoredome, departing from the Lord.
text.DRV: 1:2 The beginning to our Lord of speaking in Osee: and our Lord said to Osee: Go, take thee a wife of fornications, and make children of fornications: because the land fornicating shall fornicate from the Lord.

text.NRSV: So he went and took Gomer daughter of Diblaim, and she conceived and bore him a son.
text.Rotherham: So he went and took Gomer, daughter of Diblaim,—and she conceived and bare him a son.
text.KJV: 1:3 So he went and tooke Gomer the daughter of Diblaim, which conceiued and bare him a sonne.
text.DRV: 1:3 And he went, and took Gomer the daughter of Debelaim: and she conceived, and bare him a son.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said to him, “Name him Jezreel; for in a little while I will punish the house of Jehu for the blood of Jezreel, and I will put an end to the kingdom of the house of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto him, Call his name Jezreel: for, yet a little, and I will visit the bloodshed of Jezreel upon the house of Jehu, and will cause to cease the kingdom of the house of Israel.
text.KJV: 1:4 And the Lord said vnto him, Call his name Iezreel; for yet a little while, and I will auenge the blood of Iezreel vpon the house of Iehu, and will cause to cease the kingdome of the house of Israel.
text.DRV: 1:4 And our Lord said to him: Call his name Jezrahel: because yet a little while, and I will visit the blood of Jezrahel upon the house of Jehu, and I will make the kingdom of the house of Israel to cease.

text.NRSV: On that day I will break the bow of Israel in the valley of Jezreel.”
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass, in that day, that I will break the bow of Israel, in the vale of Jezreel.
text.KJV: 1:5 And it shall come to passe at that day, that I will breake the bow of Israel in the valley of Iezreel.
text.DRV: 1:5 And in that day I will break the bow of Israel in the valley of Jezrahel.

text.NRSV: She conceived again and bore a daughter. Then the Lord said to him, “Name her Lo-ruhamah, for I will no longer have pity on the house of Israel or forgive them.
text.Rotherham: Then conceived she again and bare a daughter, and he said to him, Call her name Lo-ruhamah ["Uncompassionated"],—for, not again any more, will I have compassion upon the house of Israel, that I should, forgive, them;
text.KJV: 1:6 ¶ And shee conceiued againe and bare a daughter, and God sayd vnto him, Call her name Lo-ruhamah: for I will no more haue mercy vpon the house of Israel: but I will vtterly take them away.
text.DRV: 1:6 And she conceived yet again, and bare a daughter, and he said to him: Call her name, Without mercy: because I will add no more to have mercy on the house of Israel, but with oblivion I will forget them.

text.NRSV: But I will have pity on the house of Judah, and I will save them by the Lord their God; I will not save them by bow, or by sword, or by war, or by horses, or by horsemen.”
text.Rotherham: but, on the house of Judah, will I have compassion, and I will save them, as Yahweh their God,—but will not save them by bow, or by sword, or by battle, by horses, or by horsemen.
text.KJV: 1:7 But I will haue mercy vpon the house of Iudah, and will saue them by the Lord their God, and will not saue them by vow, nor by sword, nor by battell, by horses nor by horsemen.
text.DRV: 1:7 And I will have mercy on the house of Juda, and will save them in the Lord their God: and I will not save them in bow and sword, and in battle, & in horses, and in horsemen.

text.NRSV: When she had weaned Lo-ruhamah, she conceived and bore a son.
text.Rotherham: And, when she had weaned Lo-ruhamah, she conceived, and bare a son.
text.KJV: 1:8 ¶ Now when shee had weaned Lo-ruhamah, she couceiued and bare a sonne.
text.DRV: 1:8 And she weaned her that was, Without mercy. And she conceived, and bare a son.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said, “Name him Lo-ammi, for you are not my people and I am not your God.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he, Call his name Lo-ammi [="No people of mine"],—for ye are Lo-ammi ["No people of mine"], and, I, will not be yours.
text.KJV: 1:9 Then sayde God, Call his name Lo-ammi: for yee are not my people, and I will not be your God.
text.DRV: 1:9 And he said: Call his name, Not my people: because you not my people, and I will not be yours.

text.NRSV: Yet the number of the people of Israel shall be like the sand of the sea, which can be neither measured nor numbered; and in the place where it was said to them, “You are not my people,” it shall be said to them, “Children of the living God.”
text.Rotherham: Yet shall the number of the sons of Israel become like the sand of the sea, which can neither he measured, nor numbered,—and it shall come to pass, in the place where it used to be said to them, No people of mine, are ye, it shall be said to them, Sons of a Living God!
text.KJV: 1:10 ¶ Yet the number of the children of Israel shall be as the sand of the sea, which cannot bee measured nor numbred, and it shall come to passe, that in the place where it was said vnto them, Yee are not my people, there it shall be said vnto them, Ye are the sonnes of the liuing God.
text.DRV: 1:10 And the number of the children of Israel shall be as the sand of the sea, that is without measure, and shall not be numbered. And it shall be in place where it shall be said to them: Not my people you: it shall be said to them: Children of the living God.

text.NRSV: The people of Judah and the people of Israel shall be gathered together, and they shall appoint for themselves one head; and they shall take possession of the land, for great shall be the day of Jezreel.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the sons of Judah and the sons of Israel, gather themselves together, as one, and shall appoint them one head, and come up out of the earth,—for great shall be the day of Jezreel.
text.KJV: 1:11 Then shall the children of Iudah and the children of Israel be gathered together, and appoint themselues one head, and they shall come vp out of the land: for great shalbe the day of Iezreel.
text.DRV: 1:11 And the children of Juda, and the children of Israel shall be gathered together: and they shall set to themselves, one head, and shall ascend out of the earth: because great is the day of Jezrahel.

text.NRSV: Say to your brother, Ammi, and to your sister, Ruhamah.
text.Rotherham: Say ye unto your brethren, O Ammi [="O my people"], and unto your sisters, O Ruhamah [="O compassionated one"]:
text.KJV: 2:1 Say ye vnto your brethren, Ammi, & to your sisters, Ruhamah:
text.DRV: 2:1 Say ye to your brethren: My people: and to your sister, She that hath obtained mercy.

text.NRSV: Plead with your mother, plead— for she is not my wife, and I am not her husband— that she put away her whoring from her face, and her adultery from between her breasts,
text.Rotherham: Contend ye with your mother, contend, for she is no wife of mine, and I am no husband of hers, Let her then put away her paramours from before her, and her partners in adultery, from her embraces:
text.KJV: 2:2 Plead with your mother, plead: for she is not my wife, neither am I her husband: let her therefore put away her whordomes out of her sight, and her adulteries from betweene her breasts;
text.DRV: 2:2 Judge your mother, judge ye: because she not my wife, and I not her husband. Let her take away fornications from her face, and her adulteries from the midst of her breasts.

text.NRSV: or I will strip her naked and expose her as on the day she was born, and make her like a wilderness, and turn her into a parched land, and kill her with thirst.
text.Rotherham: Lest I strip off her under-clothing, and set her forth to view, as in the day she was born,—and make her like a wilderness, and render her like a land that is parched, and suffer her to die of thirst;
text.KJV: 2:3 Lest I strip her naked, and set her as in the day that shee was borne, and make her as a wildernesse, and set her like a drie land, and slay her with thirst.
text.DRV: 2:3 Lest perhaps I strip her naked, and set her according to the day of her nativity: and I will lay her as a wilderness, and will set her as a land unpassable, and will kill her with drought.

text.NRSV: Upon her children also I will have no pity, because they are children of whoredom.
text.Rotherham: And, on her children, not have compassion,—because, the children of paramours, they are.
text.KJV: 2:4 And I will not haue mercy vpon her children, for they be the children of whordomes.
text.DRV: 2:4 And I will not have mercy on her children: because they are the children of fornications:

text.NRSV: For their mother has played the whore; she who conceived them has acted shamefully. For she said, “I will go after my lovers; they give me my bread and my water, my wool and my flax, my oil and my drink.”
text.Rotherham: For their mother, hath been unchaste, and she that conceived them, hath caused shame,—for she said, Let me go after my lovers! who used to give my bread, and my water, my wool, and my flax, mine oil, and my drink.
text.KJV: 2:5 For their mother hath played the harlot: shee that conceiued them hath done shamefully: for shee sayd, I will goe after my louers, that giue me my bread and my water, my wooll and my flaxe, mine oyle, and my drinke.
text.DRV: 2:5 because their mother hath fornicated, she is confounded that conceived them: because she said: I will go after my lovers, that give me my breads, & my waters, my wool, and my flax, mine oil, & my drink.

text.NRSV: Therefore I will hedge up her way with thorns; and I will build a wall against her, so that she cannot find her paths.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, behold me! hedging up her way, with thorns,—and I will wall her in, and, her footpaths, shall she not find.
text.KJV: 2:6 ¶ Therefore behold, I will hedge vp thy way with thornes, and make a wall, that she shall not find her pathes.
text.DRV: 2:6 For this behold I will hedge thy way with thorns, and I will hedge it with a wall, & she shall not find her paths.

text.NRSV: She shall pursue her lovers, but not overtake them; and she shall seek them, but shall not find them. Then she shall say, “I will go and return to my first husband, for it was better with me then than now.”
text.Rotherham: And, when she shall pursue her lovers, and not overtake them, and shall seek them and not find, then will she say, Let me go my way now! and return unto my first husband, for it was better with me, then, than, now!
text.KJV: 2:7 And she shall follow after her louers, but she shall not ouertake them, and she shall seeke them, but shall not find them: then shall she say, I will goe and returne to my first husband, for then was it better with me then now.
text.DRV: 2:7 And she shall follow her lovers, and shall not overtake them: and she shall seek them, and shall not find, and she shall say: I will go, and will return to my former husband: because it was well with me then, more than now.

text.NRSV: She did not know that it was I who gave her the grain, the wine, and the oil, and who lavished upon her silver and gold that they used for Baal.
text.Rotherham: But, she, owneth not, that, I, gave her—the corn, and the new wine and the oil,—silver, also increased I unto her, and gold—[which] they offered to Baal!
text.KJV: 2:8 For she did not know that I gaue her corne, and wine, and oyle, and multiplied her siluer and gold, which they prepared for Baal.
text.DRV: 2:8 And she did not know that I gave her corn, and wine, and oil, and multiplied silver unto her, & gold, which they have made to Baal.

text.NRSV: Therefore I will take back my grain in its time, and my wine in its season; and I will take away my wool and my flax, which were to cover her nakedness.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, will I again take away my corn, in the time thereof, and my new wine, in the season thereof,—and will recover my wool and my flax [given] to hide her shame.
text.KJV: 2:9 Therefore will I returne, and take away my Corne in the time thereof, and my wine in the season thereof, and wil recouer my wooll and my flaxe giuen to couer her nakednesse.
text.DRV: 2:9 Therefore will I return, and will take my corn in his time, and my wine in his time, and I will deliver my wool, and my flax, which covered her ignominy.

text.NRSV: Now I will uncover her shame in the sight of her lovers, and no one shall rescue her out of my hand.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, will I expose her unseemliness, before the eyes of her lovers,—and no, man, shall deliver her out of my hand!
text.KJV: 2:10 And now will I discouer her lewdnesse in the sight of her louers, and none shall deliuer her out of mine hand.
text.DRV: 2:10 And now I will reveal her folly in the eyes of her lovers: and there shall not a man deliver her out of my hand:

text.NRSV: I will put an end to all her mirth, her festivals, her new moons, her sabbaths, and all her appointed feasts.
text.Rotherham: And I will cause to cease all her mirth, her pilgrim-festival, her new moon and her sabbath,—and her every appointed meeting;
text.KJV: 2:11 I will also cause all her mirth to cease, her feast daies, her new moones, and her Sabbaths, and all her solemne feasts.
text.DRV: 2:11 and I will make all her joy to cease, her solemnity, her new-moon, her sabbath, & all her festival times.

text.NRSV: I will lay waste her vines and her fig trees, of which she said, “These are my pay, which my lovers have given me.” I will make them a forest, and the wild animals shall devour them.
text.Rotherham: And will lay waste her vine and her fig- tree, as to which she hath said, A present, are they for myself, which my lovers, have given me,—and I will make of them a thicket, and the wild beasts of the field shall devour them.
text.KJV: 2:12 And I will destroy her vines and her figge trees, whereof she hath said; These are my rewards that my louers haue giuen me: and I will make them a forrest, and the beasts of the field shall eate them.
text.DRV: 2:12 And I will destroy her vine, and her figtree, of which she said: These are my rewards, which my lovers have given me: and I will lay her as a forest, & the beast of the field shall eat her.

text.NRSV: I will punish her for the festival days of the Baals, when she offered incense to them and decked herself with her rings and jewelry, and went after her lovers, and forgot me, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So will I visit upon her the days of the Baals, unto whom she used to burn incense, and decked herself with her nose-ring and her jewelry, and went her way after her lovers,—whereas, me, she forgat, Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: 2:13 And I will visite vpon her the daies of Baalim, wherein she burnt incense to them, and she decked her selfe with her eare-rings, and her Iewels, and she went after her louers, and forgate me, saith the Lord.
text.DRV: 2:13 And I will visit upon her the days of Baalim, to whom she burnt incense, and was adorned with her earlet, and with her tablet, & went after her lovers, & forgot me, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Therefore, I will now allure her, and bring her into the wilderness, and speak tenderly to her.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, lo! I, am going to persuade her, and, though I conduct her forth into wilderness, yet will I speak unto her heart.
text.KJV: 2:14 ¶ Therefore behold, I will allure her, and bring her into the wildernesse, and speake comfortably vnto her.
text.DRV: 2:14 For this, lo I will allure her, and will lead her into the wilderness: and I will speak to her heart.

text.NRSV: From there I will give her her vineyards, and make the Valley of Achor a door of hope. There she shall respond as in the days of her youth, as at the time when she came out of the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Then will I give to her her vineyards from thence, and the vale of Achor [="trouble"] for a door of hope,—and she will respond there, as in the days of her youth, and as in the day when she came up out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: 2:15 And I wil giue her, her vineyards from thence, and the valley of Achor for a doore of hope, and she shall sing there, as in the dayes of her youth, and as in the day when she came vp out of the land of Egypt.
text.DRV: 2:15 And I will give her dressers of vines out of the same place, and the vale of Achor to open hope: and she shall sing there according to the days of her youth, and according to the days of her ascending out of the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: On that day, says the Lord, you will call me, “My husband,” and no longer will you call me, “My Baal.”
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass, in that day, Declareth Yahweh, that she will call me Ishi [="My husband"], and will not call me any more, Baali [="Mine owner"].
text.KJV: 2:16 And it shall be at that day, saith the Lord, that thou shalt call mee Ishi; and shalt call mee no more Baali.
text.DRV: 2:16 And it shall be in that day, saith our Lord: She shall call me: My husband, and she shall call me no more, Baalim.

text.NRSV: For I will remove the names of the Baals from her mouth, and they shall be mentioned by name no more.
text.Rotherham: So will I take away the names of the Baals, out of her mouth,—and they shall not be called to mind any more, by their name.
text.KJV: 2:17 For I will take away the names of Baalim out of her mouth, & they shal no more be remembred by their name.
text.DRV: 2:17 And I will take away the names of Baalim out of her mouth, and she shall no more remember their name.

text.NRSV: I will make for you a covenant on that day with the wild animals, the birds of the air, and the creeping things of the ground; and I will abolish the bow, the sword, and war from the land; and I will make you lie down in safety.
text.Rotherham: And I will solemnize to them a covenant, in that day, with the wild-beast of the field, and with the bird of the heavens, and the creeping thing of the ground,—and, bow and sword and battle, will I break in pieces out of the land, so will I cause them to lie down, in security.
text.KJV: 2:18 And in that day will I make a couenant for them with the beasts of the field, and with the foules of heauen, and with the creeping things of the ground: and I will breake the bow and the sword, and the battell out of the earth, and will make them to lie downe safely.
text.DRV: 2:18 And I will make with them a league in that day, with the beast of the field, and with the fowl of the heaven, and with that, which creepeth on the earth: and bow, and sword, and battle I will destroy out of the earth: and will make them sleep confidently.

text.NRSV: And I will take you for my wife forever; I will take you for my wife in righteousness and in justice, in steadfast love, and in mercy.
text.Rotherham: And I will take thee unto myself, unto times age-abiding,—yea I will take thee unto myself, in righteousness and in justice, and in lovingkindness, and in abounding compassion:
text.KJV: 2:19 And I will betroth thee vnto me for euer; yea, I will betroth thee vnto me in righteousnesse, and in iudgement, and in louing kindnesse, and in mercies.
text.DRV: 2:19 And I will despouse thee to me for ever: and I will despouse thee to me in justice, and judgement, and in mercy, and in commiserations.

text.NRSV: I will take you for my wife in faithfulness; and you shall know the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Yea I will take thee unto myself, in faithfulness,—So shalt thou know Yahweh.
text.KJV: 2:20 I will euen betroth thee vnto me in faithfulnesse, and thou shalt know the Lord.
text.DRV: 2:20 And I will despouse thee to me in faith: and thou shalt know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: On that day I will answer, says the Lord, I will answer the heavens and they shall answer the earth;
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass in that day, that I will respond, Declareth Yahweh, I will respond to the heavens,—and, they, shall respond to the earth;
text.KJV: 2:21 And it shall come to passe in that day, I will heare, saith the Lord, I will heare the heauens, and they shall heare the earth,
text.DRV: 2:21 And it shall be in that day: I will hear, saith our Lord, I will hear the heavens, and they shall hear the earth.

text.NRSV: and the earth shall answer the grain, the wine, and the oil, and they shall answer Jezreel;
text.Rotherham: And, the earth, shall respond to the corn, and to the new wine and to the oil,—and, they, shall respond to Jezreel [="Whom GOD scattereth," "Whom GOD soweth"].
text.KJV: 2:22 And the earth shall heare the corne, and the wine, and the oyle, and they shall heare Iezreel.
text.DRV: 2:22 And the earth shall hear wheat, and wine, and oil: and these shall hear Jezrahel.

text.NRSV: and I will sow him for myself in the land. And I will have pity on Lo-ruhamah, and I will say to Lo-ammi, “You are my people”; and he shall say, “You are my God.”
text.Rotherham: So will I sow her unto me in the land, and will have compassion upon the uncompassionated one [="Lo-ruhamah"],—and will say to him who was No-people-of-mine [="Lo-ammi"], My people, thou art, and, he, shall say, My God!
text.KJV: 2:23 And I will sow her vnto me in the earth, and I will haue mercy vpon her that had not obtained mercy, and I will say to them which were not my people; Thou art my people, and they shallsay, Thou art my God.
text.DRV: 2:23 And I will sow her unto me into the earth, and I will have mercy on her, that was without mercy.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to me again, “Go, love a woman who has a lover and is an adulteress, just as the Lord loves the people of Israel, though they turn to other gods and love raisin-cakes.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto me, Once more, go love a woman who loveth a friend, and is an adulteress,—according to the love of Yahweh unto the sons of Israel, though they keep turning away unto other gods, and love [idolatrous] raisin-cakes.
text.KJV: 3:1 Then said the Lord vnto me, Goe yet, loue a woman (beloued of her friend, yet an adulteresse) according to the loue of the Lord toward the children of Israel, who looke to other gods, and loue flagons of wine.
text.DRV: 3:1 And our Lord said to me: Yet again go, love a woman beloved of her friend, and an adulteress; as our Lord loveth the children of Israel, and they have respect to strange gods, and love the kernels of grapes.

text.NRSV: So I bought her for fifteen shekels of silver and a homer of barley and a measure of wine.
text.Rotherham: So I secured her to me, for fifteen pieces of silver,—and a homer of barley, and a half-homer of barley;
text.KJV: 3:2 So I bought her to me for fifteene pieces of siluer, and for an homer of barley and an halfe homer of barley.
text.DRV: 3:2 And I digged her unto me for fifteen pieces of silver, and for a core of barley, and for half a core of barley.

text.NRSV: And I said to her, “You must remain as mine for many days; you shall not play the whore, you shall not have intercourse with a man, nor I with you.”
text.Rotherham: and I said unto her, Many days, shalt thou tarry for me, thou shalt not be unchaste, neither shalt thou become another man’s,—moreover also, I, [will tarry] for thee.
text.KJV: 3:3 And I said vnto her, Thou shalt abide for me many dayes, thou shalt not play the harlot, & thou shalt not be for an other man, so will I also be for thee.
text.DRV: 3:3 And I said to her: Thou shalt expect me many days: thou shalt not fornicate, and thou shalt be no man's: but I also will expect thee.

text.NRSV: For the Israelites shall remain many days without king or prince, without sacrifice or pillar, without ephod or teraphim.
text.Rotherham: For, many days, shall the sons of Israel tarry, without king, and without ruler, and without sacrifice, and without pillar; and without ephod, or household gods.
text.KJV: 3:4 For the children of Israel shall abide many dayes without a King, and without a Prince, and without a sacrifice, and without an image, and without an Ephod, and without Teraphim.
text.DRV: 3:4 Because many days shall the children of Israel sit without king, and without prince, and without sacrifice, and without altar, and without ephod, and without theraphim.

text.NRSV: Afterward the Israelites shall return and seek the Lord their God, and David their king; they shall come in awe to the Lord and to his goodness in the latter days.
text.Rotherham: Afterwards, shall the sons of Israel return, and seek Yahweh their God, and David their king,—and shall turn with throbbing hearts unto Yahweh and unto his goodness, in the afterpart of the days.
text.KJV: 3:5 Afterward shall the children of Israel returne, and seeke the Lord their God, and Dauid their King, and shall feare the Lord, and his goodnesse in the latter dayes.
text.DRV: 3:5 And after this the children of Israel shall return, and shall seek the Lord their God, and David their king: and they shall dread at the Lord, and at his goodness in the last days.

text.NRSV: Hear the word of the Lord, O people of Israel; for the Lord has an indictment against the inhabitants of the land. There is no faithfulness or loyalty, and no knowledge of God in the land.
text.Rotherham: Hear the word of Yahweh, ye sons of Israel,—that, a controversy, hath Yahweh with the inhabitants of the land, because there is no faithfulness, nor lovingkindness, nor knowledge of God, in the land:
text.KJV: 4:1 Heare the worde of the Lord, yee children of Israel: for the Lord hath a controuersie with the inhabitants of the land, because there is no trueth, nor mercie, nor knowledge of God in the land.
text.DRV: 4:1 Hear the word of our Lord ye children of Israel, because there is judgement to our Lord with the inhabitants of the land: for there is no truth, and there is no mercy, and there is no knowledge of God in the land.

text.NRSV: Swearing, lying, and murder, and stealing and adultery break out; bloodshed follows bloodshed.
text.Rotherham: Cursing and lying, and killing and stealing, and committing adultery, have broken forth, and, blood-shedding, unto blood-shedding, doth extend.
text.KJV: 4:2 By swearing, and lying, and killing, and stealing, and committing adulterie, they breake out, and blood toucheth blood.
text.DRV: 4:2 Cursing, and lying, and manslaughter, & theft, and adultery have overflowed, & blood hath touched blood.

text.NRSV: Therefore the land mourns, and all who live in it languish; together with the wild animals and the birds of the air, even the fish of the sea are perishing.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, shall the land mourn, and everyone who dwelleth therein shall languish, with the wild beast of the field and with the bird of the heavens,—moreover also, the fishes of the sea, shall be withdrawn.
text.KJV: 4:3 Therefore shall the land mourne, and euery one that dwelleth therein shall languish, with the beastes of the field, and with the foules of heauen, yea the fishes of the Sea also shall be taken away.
text.DRV: 4:3 For this shall the land mourn, and every one shall be weakened that dwelleth in it, in the beast of the field, and in the fowl of the heaven: yea and the fishes of the sea shall be gathered together.

text.NRSV: Yet let no one complain, and let no one accuse, for with you is my contention, O priest.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit let, no man, contend, nor let him rebuke another,—since, thy people, are as they who contend against a priest:
text.KJV: 4:4 Yet let no man striue, nor reproue another: for this people are as they that striue with the priest.
text.DRV: 4:4 But yet let not every man judge: and let not a man be rebuked: for the people are as those, that gainsay the Priest.

text.NRSV: You shall stumble by day; the prophet also shall stumble with you by night, and I will destroy your mother.
text.Rotherham: So shouldst thou stumble in the daytime, and even the prophet stumble with thee, in the night,—and I should destroy thine own mother.
text.KJV: 4:5 Therefore shalt thou fall in the day, and the prophet also shall fall with thee in the night, and I will destroy thy mother.
text.DRV: 4:5 And thou shalt fall today, and the Prophet also shall fall with thee: in the night I made thy mother hold her peace.

text.NRSV: My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge; because you have rejected knowledge, I reject you from being a priest to me. And since you have forgotten the law of your God, I also will forget your children.
text.Rotherham: My people, are destroyed, for lack of knowledge,—Because, thou, hast rejected knowledge, therefore will I reject thee from ministering as priest unto me, and, because thou hast forgotten the law of thy God, I also, will forget thy children.
text.KJV: 4:6 ¶ My people are destroyed for lacke of knowledge: because thou hast reiected knowledge, I will also reiect thee, that thou shalt be no priest to me: seeing thou hast forgotten the lawe of thy God, I wil also forget thy children.
text.DRV: 4:6 My people have held their peace, because they had not knowledge: because thou hast repelled knowledge, I will repel thee, that thou do not the function of priesthood unto me: and thou hast forgotten the law of thy God, I also will forget thy children.

text.NRSV: The more they increased, the more they sinned against me; they changed their glory into shame.
text.Rotherham: As they were magnified, so, they sinned against me,—My glory—for what was contemptible, did they exchange.
text.KJV: 4:7 As they were increased, so they sinned against me: therfore wil I change their glory into shame.
text.DRV: 4:7 According to the multitude of them so have they sinned to me: their glory I will change into ignominy.

text.NRSV: They feed on the sin of my people; they are greedy for their iniquity.
text.Rotherham: The sin of my people, they do eat,—and. unto their iniquity, lift they up every man his desire.
text.KJV: 4:8 They eate vp the sinne of my people, and they set their heart on their iniquitie.
text.DRV: 4:8 They shall eat the sins of my people, and at their iniquity shall lift up their souls.

text.NRSV: And it shall be like people, like priest; I will punish them for their ways, and repay them for their deeds.
text.Rotherham: So doth it come to be—like people, like priest,—therefore will I visit upon him his ways, and, his doings, will I bring back to him;
text.KJV: 4:9 And there shall be like people, like priest: and I will punish them for their wayes, and reward them their doings.
text.DRV: 4:9 And as the people, so shall the Priest be: and I will visit their ways upon them, and their cogitations I will render to them.

text.NRSV: They shall eat, but not be satisfied; they shall play the whore, but not multiply; because they have abandoned the Lord to devote themselves to
text.Rotherham: And they shall eat, and not be satisfied, they have encouraged unchastity, yet have not been making increase,—for unto, Yahweh, have they left off giving heed:—
text.KJV: 4:10 For they shall eate, and not haue enough: they shall commit whordome, and shall not increase, because they haue left off to take heed to the Lord.
text.DRV: 4:10 And they shall eat and shall not be filled: they have fornicated, & have not ceased: because they have forsaken our Lord in not observing.

text.NRSV: whoredom. Wine and new wine take away the understanding.
text.Rotherham: Unchastity, and wine, and new wine, take away the heart.
text.KJV: 4:11 Whoredome, and wine, and newe wine take away the heart.
text.DRV: 4:11 Fornication, & wine, & drunkenness take away the heart.

text.NRSV: My people consult a piece of wood, and their divining rod gives them oracles. For a spirit of whoredom has led them astray, and they have played the whore, forsaking their God.
text.Rotherham: My people, of their Wood, do ask,—Let, their Staff, then tell them,—for, the spirit of unchastity, hath led them astray, and they have unchastely departed from under their God.
text.KJV: 4:12 ¶ My people aske counsel at their stocks, and their staffe declareth vnto them: for the spirit of whordomes hath caused them to erre, and they haue gone a whoring from vnder their God.
text.DRV: 4:12 My people hath asked in their wood, & their staff hath declared unto them: for the spirit of fornications hath deceived them, & they have fornicated from their God.

text.NRSV: They sacrifice on the tops of the mountains, and make offerings upon the hills, under oak, poplar, and terebinth, because their shade is good. Therefore your daughters play the whore, and your daughters-in-law commit adultery.
text.Rotherham: On the headlands of the mountains, they sacrifice, and, on the hills, burn they incense, under oak and poplar and terebinth, because, pleasant, is the shade thereof: For this cause, do your daughters, become unchaste, and, your brides, commit adultery.
text.KJV: 4:13 They sacrifice vpon the tops of the mountaines, and burne incense vpon the hilles vnder okes and poplars, and elmes, because the shadowe thereof is good: therefore your daughters shall commit whoredome, and your spouses shall commit adulterie.
text.DRV: 4:13 Upon the heads of mountains they did sacrifice, and upon little hills they burnt incense: under the oak, and the poplar-tree, and the terebinth, because the shadow thereof was good: therefore shall your daughters fornicate, and your spouses shall be adulteresses.

text.NRSV: I will not punish your daughters when they play the whore, nor your daughters-in-law when they commit adultery; for the men themselves go aside with whores, and sacrifice with temple prostitutes; thus a people without understanding comes to ruin.
text.Rotherham: I do not bring punishment upon your daughters when they become unchaste, nor upon your brides, when they commit adultery, for, the men themselves, with unchaste women, do seclude themselves, and, with the common women of the shrine, do offer sacrifice,—and, a people who will not discern, must be ruined.
text.KJV: 4:14 I will not punish your daughters when they commit whordome, nor your spouses when they commit adulterie: for themselues are separated with whores, and they sacrifice with harlots: therfore the people that doth not vnderstand, shall fall.
text.DRV: 4:14 I will not visit upon your daughters when they shall fornicate, and upon your spouses when they shall commit adultery: because they themselves converst with harlots, and with the effeminate they did sacrifice, and the people not understanding shall be beaten.

text.NRSV: Though you play the whore, O Israel, do not let Judah become guilty. Do not enter Gilgal, or go up to Beth-aven, and do not swear, “As the Lord lives.”
text.Rotherham: Though unchaste art thou, O Israel, let not Judah, become guilty, neither let them enter Gilgal, nor go up to Beth-aven, and [then] swear, By the life of Yahweh!
text.KJV: 4:15 ¶ Though thou Israel play the harlot, yet let not Iudah offend, and come not ye vnto Gilgal, neither goe ye vp to Beth-auen, nor sweare, The Lord liueth:
text.DRV: 4:15 If thou fornicate o Israel, at the least let not Juda offend: and enter ye not into Galgal, and go not up into Bethaven, neither swear ye: Our Lord liveth.

text.NRSV: Like a stubborn heifer, Israel is stubborn; can the Lord now feed them like a lamb in a broad pasture?
text.Rotherham: For, as a heifer that is stubborn, hath Israel, been stubborn,—Now, can Yahweh, turn them out to pasture, like a young ram in a wide place?
text.KJV: 4:16 For Israel slideth backe, as a backe sliding heifer: now the Lord will feede them as a lambe in a large place.
text.DRV: 4:16 Because Israel hath declined as a wanton cow: now will our Lord feed them, as a lamb in latitude.

text.NRSV: Ephraim is joined to idols—let him alone.
text.Rotherham: Mated with idols, is Ephraim, let him alone.
text.KJV: 4:17 Ephraim is ioyned to idoles: let him alone.
text.DRV: 4:17 Ephraim is partaker of idols, let him alone.

text.NRSV: When their drinking is over, they indulge in whoredom; they love lewdness more than their glory.
text.Rotherham: Their drinking-bout, having passed,—they became, unchaste, they loved wildly, a contempt, became her great men.
text.KJV: 4:18 Their drinke is sowre: they haue committed whordome continually: her rulers with shame doe loue, Giue ye.
text.DRV: 4:18 Their banquet is separated, with fornication they have fornicated: the protectors thereof loved to bring ignominy.

text.NRSV: A wind has wrapped them in its wings, and they shall be ashamed because of their sacrifices.
text.Rotherham: The wind hath bound her up in its wings,—that they may be ashamed, because of their sacrifices.
text.KJV: 4:19 The wind hath bound her vp in her wings, and they shall be ashamed because of their sacrifices.
text.DRV: 4:19 The spirit hath bound him in his wings, and they shall be confounded at their sacrifices.

text.NRSV: Hear this, O priests! Give heed, O house of Israel! Listen, O house of the king! For the judgment pertains to you; for you have been a snare at Mizpah, and a net spread upon Tabor,
text.Rotherham: Hear ye this—O priests, and attend, O house of Israel, and ye, House of the King, give ear, for, to you, pertaineth the sentence,—for, a snare, have ye been to Mizpah, and a net spread on Tabor.
text.KJV: 5:1 Heare yee this, O priests, and hearken, ye house of Israel, and giue yee eare, O house of the king: for iudgement is toward you, because yee haue beene a snare on Mizpah, and a net spread vpon Tabor.
text.DRV: 5:1 Hear ye this o Priests, and attend ye house of Israel, and you the king's house harken: because there is judgement for you, because you are become a snare to speculation, & a net spread upon Thabor.

text.NRSV: and a pit dug deep in Shittim; but I will punish all of them.
text.Rotherham: And, a slaughter, have apostates deeply designed,—though, I, was a rebuker to them all.
text.KJV: 5:2 And the reuolters are profound to make slaughter, though I haue bene a rebuker of them all.
text.DRV: 5:2 And victims you have declined into the depth: & I the teacher of them all.

text.NRSV: I know Ephraim, and Israel is not hidden from me; for now, O Ephraim, you have played the whore; Israel is defiled.
text.Rotherham: I, have known Ephraim, and, Israel, hath not been hidden from me,—for, now, hast thou committed unchastity, O Ephraim, Israel, hath made himself impure.
text.KJV: 5:3 I know Ephraim, and Israel is not hid from me: for now, O Ephraim, thou committest whordome, and Israel is defiled.
text.DRV: 5:3 I know Ephraim, & Israel is not hid from me: because now hath Ephraim fornicated, Israel is contaminated.

text.NRSV: Their deeds do not permit them to return to their God. For the spirit of whoredom is within them, and they do not know the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Their doings, will not suffer, them to return unto their God,—for, the spirit of unchastity, is within them, and, Yahweh, have they not known.
text.KJV: 5:4 They will not frame their doings to turne vnto their God: for the spirit of whoredomes is in the midst of them, and they haue not knowen the Lord.
text.DRV: 5:4 They will not give their cogitations to return to their God, because the spirit of fornications is in the midst of them, & they have not known the Lord.

text.NRSV: Israel’s pride testifies against him; Ephraim stumbles in his guilt; Judah also stumbles with them.
text.Rotherham: Therefore will the Excellency of Israel, answer, to his face,—and, Israel and Ephraim, shall stumble in their iniquity, even Judah with them, hath stumbled.
text.KJV: 5:5 And the pride of Israel doth testifie to his face: therefore shall Israel and Ephraim fall in their iniquity: Iudah also shall fall with them.
text.DRV: 5:5 And the arrogancy of Israel shall answer in his face: and Israel and Ephraim shall fall in their iniquity, Judas also shall fall with them.

text.NRSV: With their flocks and herds they shall go to seek the Lord, but they will not find him; he has withdrawn from them.
text.Rotherham: With their flocks and with their herds, will they go to seek Yahweh, but shall not find him; he hath withdrawn himself from them.
text.KJV: 5:6 They shall goe with their flocks, and with their heards to seeke the Lord: but they shall not finde him, he hath withdrawen himselfe from them.
text.DRV: 5:6 In their flocks and in their herds they shall go to seek the Lord, and shall not find: he is taken away from them.

text.NRSV: They have dealt faithlessly with the Lord; for they have borne illegitimate children. Now the new moon shall devour them along with their parcels of land.
text.Rotherham: With Yahweh, have they dealt treacherously, for, to alien children, have they given birth,—now, a new moon, shall devour them, with their portions.
text.KJV: 5:7 They haue dealt treacherously against the Lord: for they haue begotten strange children, now shall a moneth deuoure them with their portions.
text.DRV: 5:7 They have prevaricated against the Lord, because they have begotten strange children: now shall a month devour them with their parts.

text.NRSV: Blow the horn in Gibeah, the trumpet in Ramah. Sound the alarm at Beth-aven; look behind you, Benjamin!
text.Rotherham: Blow ye a horn in Gibeah, a trumpet in Ramah,—sound an alarm at Beth-aven, behind thee, O Benjamin!
text.KJV: 5:8 Blow yee the cornet in Gibeah, and the trumpet in Ramah: cry alowd at Beth-auen: after thee, O Beniamin.
text.DRV: 5:8 Sound with the trumpet in Gabaa, & with the shalm in Rama: howl ye in Bethaven, behind thy back o Benjamin.

text.NRSV: Ephraim shall become a desolation on the day of punishment; among the tribes of Israel I declare what is sure.
text.Rotherham: Ephraim, shall become, a desolation, in the day of rebuke: Throughout the tribes of Israel, have I hide known what is sure.
text.KJV: 5:9 Ephraim shall be desolate in the day of rebuke: among the tribes of Israel haue I made knowen that which shall surely be.
text.DRV: 5:9 Ephraim shall be in desolation in the day of correction: in the tribes of Israel I have shewed faith.

text.NRSV: The princes of Judah have become like those who remove the landmark; on them I will pour out my wrath like water.
text.Rotherham: The rulers of Judah have become as they who remove a land-mark. Upon them, will I pour out, like water, my wrath.
text.KJV: 5:10 The Princes of Iudah were like them that remooue the bound: therefore I will powre out my wrath vpon them like water.
text.DRV: 5:10 The princes of Juda are become as they that take the bound: I will pour out my wrath as water upon them.

text.NRSV: Ephraim is oppressed, crushed in judgment, because he was determined to go after vanity.
text.Rotherham: Oppressed, is Ephraim, crushed in judgment,—because he hath, wilfully, walked after falsehood.
text.KJV: 5:11 Ephraim is oppressed, and broken in iudgement: because he willingly walked after the commandement.
text.DRV: 5:11 Ephraim is suffering calumny, broken in judgement: because he began to go after filthiness.

text.NRSV: Therefore I am like maggots to Ephraim, and like rottenness to the house of Judah.
text.Rotherham: But, I, was like a moth, to Ephraim,—and like rotten wood to the house of Judah.
text.KJV: 5:12 Therefore wil I be vnto Ephraim as a moth: and to the house of Iudah as rottennesse.
text.DRV: 5:12 And I as it were a moth to Ephraim: & as the rot to the house of Juda.

text.NRSV: When Ephraim saw his sickness, and Judah his wound, then Ephraim went to Assyria, and sent to the great king. But he is not able to cure you or heal your wound.
text.Rotherham: When Ephraim, saw, his injury, and Judah his wound, then went Ephraim unto Assyria, and [Judah] sent unto a hostile king,—yet, he, cannot heal you, nor will the wound, remove from you.
text.KJV: 5:13 When Ephraim saw his sicknesse, and Iudah saw his wound: then went Ephraim to the Assyrian, and sent to king Iareb; yet could he not heale you, nor cure you of your wound.
text.DRV: 5:13 And Ephraim saw his sickness, and Judas his band: and Ephraim went to Assur, and sent to the king revenger: and he shall not be able to heal you, neither shall he be able to loose the band from you.

text.NRSV: For I will be like a lion to Ephraim, and like a young lion to the house of Judah. I myself will tear and go away; I will carry off, and no one shall rescue.
text.Rotherham: For, I, will be as a lion unto Ephraim, and as a young lion to the house of Judah,—I, I, will tear in pieces, and depart, I will carry off, and none be able to rescue.
text.KJV: 5:14 For I will bee vnto Ephraim as a Lion, and as a yong Lion to the house of Iudah: I, euen I wil teare and goe away: I will take away, and none shall rescue him.
text.DRV: 5:14 Because I as it were a lioness to Ephraim, and as a lion's whelp to the house of Juda: I, I will take, and go: I will take away, and there is none that can deliver.

text.NRSV: I will return again to my place until they acknowledge their guilt and seek my face; in their distress they will beg my favor.
text.Rotherham: I will depart, will return unto my place! till what time they acknowledge their guilt, and seek my face,—In their trouble, will they make for me diligent search.
text.KJV: 5:15 ¶ I will goe and returne to my place, till they acknowledge their offence, and seeke my face: in their affliction they will seeke me early.
text.DRV: 5:15 Going I will return to my place: until you fail and seek my face.

text.NRSV: “Come, let us return to the Lord; for it is he who has torn, and he will heal us; he has struck down, and he will bind us up.
text.Rotherham: Come, and let us return unto Yahweh! for, he, hath torn, that he might heal us,—smitten, that he might bind us up.
text.KJV: 6:1 Come, and let vs returne vnto the Lord: for hee hath torne, and hee will heale vs: he hath smitten, and he will binde vs vp.
text.DRV: 6:1 In their tribulation early they will rise up to me: Come, & let us return to our Lord.

text.NRSV: After two days he will revive us; on the third day he will raise us up, that we may live before him.
text.Rotherham: He will bring us to life, after two days,—on the third day, will he raise us up, that we may live before him.
text.KJV: 6:2 After two daies will he reuiue vs, in the third day he will raise vs vp, and we shall liue in his sight.
text.DRV: 6:2 Because he hath wounded, & will heal us: he will strike, & will cure us.

text.NRSV: Let us know, let us press on to know the Lord; his appearing is as sure as the dawn; he will come to us like the showers, like the spring rains that water the earth.”
text.Rotherham: Then let us know—let us press on to know—Yahweh, Like the dawn, is his coming forth assured,—that he may come like a down-pour upon us, like the harvest-rain, [and] the seed-rain of the land.
text.KJV: 6:3 Then shal we know, if we follow on to know the Lord: his going forth is prepared, as the morning; & he shall come vnto vs, as the raine; as the latter and former raine vnto the earth.
text.DRV: 6:3 He will revive us after two days: in the third day he will raise us up, & we shall live in his sight. We shall know, & we shall follow, that we may know our Lord. As the morning light, is his coming forth prepared, and he will come to us as a shower timely, and late to the earth.

text.NRSV: What shall I do with you, O Ephraim? What shall I do with you, O Judah? Your love is like a morning cloud, like the dew that goes away early.
text.Rotherham: What can I do unto thee, O Ephraim? What can I do unto thee, O Judah? for, your lovingkindness, is like a morning cloud, yea, like the dew, early departing!
text.KJV: 6:4 ¶ O Ephraim, what shall I doe vnto thee? O Iudah, what shall I do vnto thee? for your goodnesse is as a morning cloud, and as the early dew it goeth away.
text.DRV: 6:4 What shall I do to thee Ephraim? What shall I do to thee Juda? Your mercy as a morning cloud, & as the dew passing away in the morning.

text.NRSV: Therefore I have hewn them by the prophets, I have killed them by the words of my mouth, and my judgment goes forth as the light.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, have I hewn them in pieces by the prophets, I have slain them by the sayings of my mouth,—and, my justice, as a light goeth forth.
text.KJV: 6:5 Therefore haue I shewed them by the Prophets: I haue slaine them by the wordes of my mouth, and thy iudgements are as the light that goeth foorth.
text.DRV: 6:5 For this have I hewed in Prophets, I have killed them in the words of my mouth: and thy judgements shall come forth as the light:

text.NRSV: For I desire steadfast love and not sacrifice, the knowledge of God rather than burnt offerings.
text.Rotherham: For, lovingkindness, I desired, and not sacrifice,—and the knowledge of God, more than ascending-offerings.
text.KJV: 6:6 For I desired mercie, and not sacrifice; and the knowledge of God more then burnt offerings.
text.DRV: 6:6 because I would mercy, and not sacrifice: and the knowledge of God more than holocausts.

text.NRSV: But at Adam they transgressed the covenant; there they dealt faithlessly with me.
text.Rotherham: But, they, like Adam, have transgressed a covenant,—There, have they dealt treacherously with me.
text.KJV: 6:7 But they like men haue transgressed the Couenant: there haue they dealt treacherously against me.
text.DRV: 6:7 But they as Adam have transgressed the covenant, there have they prevaricated against me.

text.NRSV: Gilead is a city of evildoers, tracked with blood.
text.Rotherham: Gilead, is a city of workers of iniquity,—tracked with blood.
text.KJV: 6:8 Gilead is a city of them that worke iniquitie; and is polluted with blood.
text.DRV: 6:8 Galaad a city of them that work idol, supplanted with blood.

text.NRSV: As robbers lie in wait for someone, so the priests are banded together; they murder on the road to Shechem, they commit a monstrous crime.
text.Rotherham: And, like liers in wait for a man, in troops, is a band of priests, on the road, will they murder towards Shechem,—because, a shameful deed, they have done.
text.KJV: 6:9 And as troupes of robbers waite for a man, so the company of priestes murther in the way by consent: for they commit lewdnesse.
text.DRV: 6:9 And as it were the jaws of men that are robbers, partaker with the Priests, of them that in the way kill those that pass out of Sichem: because they have wrought wickedness.

text.NRSV: In the house of Israel I have seen a horrible thing; Ephraim’s whoredom is there, Israel is defiled.
text.Rotherham: In the house of Israel, have I seen a horrible thing,—there, the unchastity of Ephraim, defiled is Israel.
text.KJV: 6:10 I haue seene an horrible thing in the house of Israel: there is the whoredome of Ephraim, Israel is defiled.
text.DRV: 6:10 In the house of Israel I saw an horrible thing: there the fornications of Ephraim: Israel is contaminated.

text.NRSV: For you also, O Judah, a harvest is appointed. When I would restore the fortunes of my people,
text.Rotherham: Judah too! a harvest is appointed for thee,—in that I will bring back the captivity of my people.
text.KJV: 6:11 Also O Iudah, hee hath set an haruest for thee, when I returned the captiuitie of my people.
text.DRV: 6:11 Yea and thou Juda put thee an harvest, when I shall convert the captivity of my people.

text.NRSV: when I would heal Israel, the corruption of Ephraim is revealed, and the wicked deeds of Samaria; for they deal falsely, the thief breaks in, and the bandits raid outside.
text.Rotherham: When I would have brought healing to Israel, then was disclosed the iniquity of Ephraim, and the wicked doings of Samaria, for they have wrought falsehood,—when, a thief, would enter, a band roamed about, outside,
text.KJV: 7:1 When I would haue healed Israel, then the iniquitie of Ephraim was discouered, and the wickednesse of Samaria: for they commit falsehood: and the thiefe commeth in, and the troupe of robbers spoileth without.
text.DRV: 7:1 When I would heal Israel, the iniquity of Ephraim was revealed, & the malice of Samaria, because they have wrought lying, & the thief hath entered in spoiling, the robber without.

text.NRSV: But they do not consider that I remember all their wickedness. Now their deeds surround them, they are before my face.
text.Rotherham: And they say not to their own hearts, that, all their wickedness, I remember, now, have their doings, beset them about, right before my face, have they been done.
text.KJV: 7:2 And they consider not in their hearts that I remember al their wickednesse: now their owne doings haue beset them about, they are before my face.
text.DRV: 7:2 And lest perhaps they may say in their hearts, that I have remembered all their malice: now have their own inventions compassed them, they have been done before me.

text.NRSV: By their wickedness they make the king glad, and the officials by their treachery.
text.Rotherham: By their wickedness, they gladden a king, and, by their flatteries,—rulers.
text.KJV: 7:3 They make the king glad with their wickednesse, and the princes with their lies.
text.DRV: 7:3 In their malice they have rejoiced the king: and in their lies the princes.

text.NRSV: They are all adulterers; they are like a heated oven, whose baker desists from stirring the fire, from the kneading of the dough until it is leavened.
text.Rotherham: They all, are adulterers, like an oven too hot for the baker,—who leaveth off stoking, after kneading the dough, till the whole be leavened.
text.KJV: 7:4 They are al adulterers, as an ouen heated by the baker: who ceaseth from raising after he hath kneaded the dough, vntill it be leauened.
text.DRV: 7:4 All they committing adultery, as it were an oven heated of the baker: the city was quiet a little from the commistion of leaven, till the whole was leavened.

text.NRSV: On the day of our king the officials became sick with the heat of wine; he stretched out his hand with mockers.
text.Rotherham: In the day of our king, the rulers, have made themselves ill, with the heat of wine,—he hath extended his hand with scoffers.
text.KJV: 7:5 In the day of our King, the princes haue made him sicke with bottels of wine, he stretched out his hand with scorners.
text.DRV: 7:5 The day of our king, the princes began to rage by reason of wine: he stretched out his hand with the scorners.

text.NRSV: For they are kindled like an oven, their heart burns within them; all night their anger smolders; in the morning it blazes like a flaming fire.
text.Rotherham: For they have made ready, like an oven, their heart, by their lying in wait,—all the night, their baker sleepeth, in the morning, he, kindleth up as it were a blazing fire.
text.KJV: 7:6 For they haue made ready their heart like an ouen, whiles they lie in wait: their baker sleepeth all the night, in the morning it burneth as a flaming fire.
text.DRV: 7:6 Because they have applied their heart as an oven, when he lay in wait for them: he slept all the night baking them, in the morning himself heated as a fire of flame.

text.NRSV: All of them are hot as an oven, and they devour their rulers. All their kings have fallen; none of them calls upon me.
text.Rotherham: They all, become hot as an oven, and devour their judges,—all their kings, have fallen, there hath been none among them crying unto me.
text.KJV: 7:7 They are all hot as an ouen, and haue deuoured their Iudges; all their Kings are fallen, there is none among them that calleth vnto me.
text.DRV: 7:7 All were heated as an oven, and have devoured their judges: all their kings are fallen: there is none amongst them that cryeth unto me.

text.NRSV: Ephraim mixes himself with the peoples; Ephraim is a cake not turned.
text.Rotherham: As for Ephraim! with the peoples, hath he been mingling himself,—Ephraim, is a cake not turned.
text.KJV: 7:8 Ephraim, he hath mixed himselfe among the people, Ephraim is a cake not turned.
text.DRV: 7:8 Ephraim himself was mingled in the peoples: Ephraim is become as hearth-baken-bread that is not turned.

text.NRSV: Foreigners devour his strength, but he does not know it; gray hairs are sprinkled upon him, but he does not know it.
text.Rotherham: Foreigners have, eaten up, his strength, and, he, knoweth it not,—even gray hairs, are sprinkled upon him, and, he, knoweth it not.
text.KJV: 7:9 Strangers haue deuoured his strength, and hee knoweth it not: yea, gray haires are here and there vpon him, yet he knoweth not.
text.DRV: 7:9 Strangers have eaten his strength, & he knew not: yea hoar hairs also were poured out on him, & he was ignorant.

text.NRSV: Israel’s pride testifies against him; yet they do not return to the Lord their God, or seek him, for all this.
text.Rotherham: Therefore doth the Excellency of Israel, answer, to his face; yet have they not returned unto Yahweh their God, nor have they sought him, in spite of all this!
text.KJV: 7:10 And the pride of Israel testifieth to his face, and they doe not returne to the Lord their God, nor seeke him for all this.
text.DRV: 7:10 And the pride of Israel shall be humbled in his face: neither did they return to the Lord their God, & they have not sought him in all these.

text.NRSV: Ephraim has become like a dove, silly and without sense; they call upon Egypt, they go to Assyria.
text.Rotherham: So then, Ephraim, hath become, like a simple dove, having no understanding, on Egypt, have they called, to Assyria, have they gone,
text.KJV: 7:11 ¶ Ephraim also is like a silly doue, without heart: they call to Egypt; they goe to Assyria.
text.DRV: 7:11 And Ephraim is become as a dove seduced, not having an heart: they invocated Ægypt, they went to the Assyrians.

text.NRSV: As they go, I will cast my net over them; I will bring them down like birds of the air; I will discipline them according to the report made to their assembly.
text.Rotherham: Whithersoever they go, I will spread over them my net, like a bird of the heavens, will I bring them down, I will chastise them, by the time the report can reach the flock of them.
text.KJV: 7:12 When they shall goe, I wil spread my net vpon them, I will bring them downe as the foules of the heauen: I will chastise them as their congregation hath heard.
text.DRV: 7:12 And when they shall go forth, I will spread my net upon them: as a fowl of the heaven will I pluck them down, I will beat them according to the hearing of their assembly.

text.NRSV: Woe to them, for they have strayed from me! Destruction to them, for they have rebelled against me! I would redeem them, but they speak lies against me.
text.Rotherham: Woe to them! for they have taken flight from me, destruction to them! for they have transgressed against me,—when, I, would have ransomed them, then, they, spake—concerning me—falsehoods.
text.KJV: 7:13 Woe vnto them, for they haue fled from me: destruction vnto them, because they haue transgressed against me, though I haue redeemed them, yet they haue spoken lies against me.
text.DRV: 7:13 Woe to them, because they have revolted from me: they shall be wasted because they have prevaricated against me: & I redeemed them & they have spoken lies against me.

text.NRSV: They do not cry to me from the heart, but they wail upon their beds; for grain and wine they gash themselves, they rebel against me.
text.Rotherham: Neither made they outcry unto me, in their heart, although they kept on howling upon their beds, over corn and new wine, they gathered themselves together, they rebelled against me.
text.KJV: 7:14 And they haue not cryed vnto me with their heart, when they howled vpon their beds: they assemble themselues for corne and wine, and they rebell against me.
text.DRV: 7:14 And they have not cried to me in their heart, but they howled in their chambers: upon wheat and wine they chewed the cud, they are revolted from me.

text.NRSV: It was I who trained and strengthened their arms, yet they plot evil against me.
text.Rotherham: When, I, had warned them, I strengthened their arm,—yet, against me, kept they on devising wickedness.
text.KJV: 7:15 Though I haue bound, and strengthened their armes, yet doe they imagine mischiefe against me.
text.DRV: 7:15 And I have taught them, and strengthened their arms: and against me they have thought malice.

text.NRSV: They turn to that which does not profit; they have become like a treacherous bow; their officials shall fall by the sword because of the rage of their tongue. This shall be their derision in the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: They would return—not to him who is on high! They have become like a deceitful bow, their rulers, shall fall by the sword, for the rage of their tongue, this, [shall be] their derision in the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: 7:16 They returne, but not to the most High: they are like a deceitfull bow: their princes shall fall by the sword, for the rage of their tongue: this shall be their derision in the land of Egypt.
text.DRV: 7:16 They returned, that they might be without yoke: they became as a deceitful bow: their princes shall fall by the sword, for the fury of their tongue. This is their scorning in the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: Set the trumpet to your lips! One like a vulture is over the house of the Lord, because they have broken my covenant, and transgressed my law.
text.Rotherham: To thy mouth, with a horn! Like an eagle, on the house of Yahweh,—because they have violated my covenant, and, against my law, have they transgressed.
text.KJV: 8:1 Set the Trumpet to thy mouth: hee shall come as an Eagle against the house of the Lord, because they haue transgressed my Couenant, and trespassed against my Lawe.
text.DRV: 8:1 In thy throat let there be a trumpet as an eagle upon the house of the Lord: for that they have transgressed my covenant, and have prevaricated my law.

text.NRSV: Israel cries to me, “My God, we know you!”
text.Rotherham: Unto me, shall they make outcry, My God! we acknowledge thee—[we], Israel!
text.KJV: 8:2 Israel shall crie vnto me, My God, we know thee.
text.DRV: 8:2 Me they shall invocate: My God, we Israel have known thee.

text.NRSV: Israel has spurned the good; the enemy shall pursue him.
text.Rotherham: Israel hath cast away what is good,—an enemy, shall pursue him.
text.KJV: 8:3 Israel hath cast off the thing that is good: the enemie shall pursue him.
text.DRV: 8:3 Israel hath cast away the good thing, the enemy shall persecute him.

text.NRSV: They made kings, but not through me; they set up princes, but without my knowledge. With their silver and gold they made idols for their own destruction.
text.Rotherham: They, have appointed kings, but not from me, have made rulers, but I have not acknowledged them: of their silver and their gold, they made themselves idols, to the end they might be cut off.
text.KJV: 8:4 They haue set vp Kings, but not by me: they haue made Princes, and I knew it not: of their siluer and their golde haue they made them idoles, that they may be cut off.
text.DRV: 8:4 They have reigned, & not of me: they have been Princes, & I knew not: their silver & their gold they made idols to themselves, that they might perish.

text.NRSV: Your calf is rejected, O Samaria. My anger burns against them. How long will they be incapable of innocence?
text.Rotherham: He hath cast away thy calf, O Samaria, kindled is mine anger upon them,—How long shall they not endure to be innocent?
text.KJV: 8:5 ¶ Thy calfe, O Samaria, hath cast thee off: mine anger is kindled against them: how long will it bee ere they attaine to innocencie?
text.DRV: 8:5 Thy calf is cast off o Samaria, my fury is wrath against them. How long can they not be cleansed?

text.NRSV: For it is from Israel, an artisan made it; it is not God. The calf of Samaria shall be broken to pieces.
text.Rotherham: For, of Israel, is even that thing! A craftsman, made it, and, a No-god, it is! For, into fragments, shall the Calf of Samaria be broken.
text.KJV: 8:6 For from Israel was it also, the workeman made it, therefore it is not God: but the calfe of Samaria shall be broken in pieces.
text.DRV: 8:6 Because it-self also is of Israel: the workman made it, and it is not God: because the calf of Samaria shall be as spiders' webs.

text.NRSV: For they sow the wind, and they shall reap the whirlwind. The standing grain has no heads, it shall yield no meal; if it were to yield, foreigners would devour it.
text.Rotherham: For, to the wind, they sow, and, to the whirlwind, they reap: stalk, hath it none, That which shooteth forth, shall yield no meal, If so be it yield, foreigners, swallow it lip,
text.KJV: 8:7 For they haue sowen the winde, and they shall reape the whirlewinde: it hath no stalke: the budde shall yeeld no meale: if so be it yeeld, the strangers shall swallow it vp.
text.DRV: 8:7 Because they shall sow wind, & reap a whirlwind: there is no standing stalk in it, the bud shall not yield meal: & if it do yield, strangers shall eat it.

text.NRSV: Israel is swallowed up; now they are among the nations as a useless vessel.
text.Rotherham: Swallowed up, is Israel; Now, have they gone among the nations, like a vessel in which no man taketh, delight.
text.KJV: 8:8 Israel is swallowed vp, now shal they be among the Gentiles, as a vessell wherein is no pleasure.
text.DRV: 8:8 Israel is devoured: Now is he become in nations as an unclean vessel.

text.NRSV: For they have gone up to Assyria, a wild ass wandering alone; Ephraim has hired lovers.
text.Rotherham: For, they, have gone up to Assyria, A wild ass going alone for himself, is Ephraim! They have hired lovers!
text.KJV: 8:9 For they are gone vp to Assyria, a wilde Asse alone by himselfe; Ephraim hath hired louers.
text.DRV: 8:9 Because they went up to Assur, a wild ass solitary to himself: Ephraim have given gifts to lovers.

text.NRSV: Though they hire allies among the nations, I will soon gather them up; and they shall soon begin to languish under the burden of kings and princes.
text.Rotherham: Even though they hire them among the nations, at once, will I gather them, when they have begun to be diminished by reason of the burden of the king of rulers.
text.KJV: 8:10 Yea, though they haue hired among the nations, now will I gather them, and they shall sorrow a little for the burden of the King of princes.
text.DRV: 8:10 Yea and when they shall hire the nations with reward, now will I gather them together: & they shall rest a while from the burden of the king and the princes.

text.NRSV: When Ephraim multiplied altars to expiate sin, they became to him altars for sinning.
text.Rotherham: Because Ephraim hath multiplied altars sinfully, they have become to him the altars of sin.
text.KJV: 8:11 Because Ephraim hath made many altars to sinne, altars shall be vnto him to sinne.
text.DRV: 8:11 Because Ephraim hath multiplied altars to sin: altars are made to him unto sin.

text.NRSV: Though I write for him the multitude of my instructions, they are regarded as a strange thing.
text.Rotherham: I have been wont to write for him the myriad things of my law,—Like something alien, have they been accounted.
text.KJV: 8:12 I haue written to him the great things of my Law, but they were counted as a strange thing.
text.DRV: 8:12 I will write to him my manifold laws, which have been accounted as strange.

text.NRSV: Though they offer choice sacrifices, though they eat flesh, the Lord does not accept them. Now he will remember their iniquity, and punish their sins; they shall return to Egypt.
text.Rotherham: My sacrificial gifts, have they been sacrificing as [common] flesh, and have eaten, Yahweh, hath not accepted them,—Now, will he call to mind their iniquity, that he may punish their sin, they, to Egypt, will return.
text.KJV: 8:13 They sacrifice flesh for the sacrifices of mine offerings, and eate it; but the Lord accepteth them not: now will he remember their iniquitie, and visite their sinnes: they shal returne to Egypt.
text.DRV: 8:13 They shall offer hosts, they shall immolate flesh, and shall eat, and the Lord will not receive them: now will he remember their iniquity, and will visit their sins: they shall return into Ægypt.

text.NRSV: For Israel has forgotten his Maker, and built palaces; and Judah has multiplied fortified cities; but I will send a fire upon his cities, and it shall devour his strongholds.
text.Rotherham: And so Israel hath forgotten his Maker, and hath built temples, and, Judah, hath multiplied fortified cities,—Therefore will I send a fire upon his cities, and it shall consume the palaces thereof.
text.KJV: 8:14 For Israel hath forgotten his maker, and buildeth temples; and Iudah hath multiplied fenced cities: but I will send a fire vpon his cities, and it shall deuoure the palaces thereof.
text.DRV: 8:14 And Israel hath forgotten his maker, and hath built temples: and Judas hath multiplied fenced cities: & I will cast fire on his cities, and it shall devour the houses thereof.

text.NRSV: Do not rejoice, O Israel! Do not exult as other nations do; for you have played the whore, departing from your God. You have loved a prostitute’s pay on all threshing floors.
text.Rotherham: Do not rejoice, O Israel, with exultation, like the peoples, for thou hast gone away unchastely from beside thy God,—thou hast loved a present, upon all the threshing-floors of corn!
text.KJV: 9:1 Reioyce not, O Israel, for ioy as other people: for thou hast gone a whoring from thy God, thou hast loued a reward vpon euery corne floore.
text.DRV: 9:1 Be not glad Israel, rejoice not as the peoples: because thou hast fornicated from thy God, thou hast loved reward above all the floors of wheat.

text.NRSV: Threshing floor and wine press shall not feed them, and the new wine shall fail them.
text.Rotherham: Threshing-floor and wine-vat, will not feed them, and, new wine, will deny them.
text.KJV: 9:2 The floore and the winepresse shall not feede them, and the new wine shall faile in her.
text.DRV: 9:2 The floor and the winepress shall not feed them, and the wine shall lie to them.

text.NRSV: They shall not remain in the land of the Lord; but Ephraim shall return to Egypt, and in Assyria they shall eat unclean food.
text.Rotherham: They shall not dwell in the land of Yahweh, but Ephraim shall return to Egypt, and, in Assyria—that which is unclean, shall they eat.
text.KJV: 9:3 They shal not dwel in þ Lords land: but Ephraim shall returne to Egypt, and they shall eat vncleane things in Assyria.
text.DRV: 9:3 They shall not dwell in the land of our Lord: Ephraim is returned into Ægypt, and among the Assyrians he hath eaten the thing polluted.

text.NRSV: They shall not pour drink offerings of wine to the Lord, and their sacrifices shall not please him. Such sacrifices shall be like mourners’ bread; all who eat of it shall be defiled; for their bread shall be for their hunger only; it shall not come into the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: They shall not pour out to Yahweh—wine, neither shall they be pleasing to him, their sacrifices, are as the food of mourning to them, all that eat thereof, shall defile themselves, because, their food for their appetite, entereth not into the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 9:4 They shall not offer wine offrings to the Lord: neither shall they be pleasing vnto him: their sacrifices shalbe vnto them as the bread of mourners: all that eate thereof shall be polluted: for their bread for their soule shall not come into the house of the Lord.
text.DRV: 9:4 They shall not offer wine to our Lord, and they shall not please him: their sacrifices as the bread of mourners: all that shall eat it shall be contaminated: because their bread for their soul, shall not enter into the house of our Lord.

text.NRSV: What will you do on the day of appointed festival, and on the day of the feast of the Lord?
text.Rotherham: What will ye do, for the day of appointed meeting? and in the day of the festival of Yahweh?
text.KJV: 9:5 What will yee doe in the solemne day, and in the day of the feast of the Lord ?
text.DRV: 9:5 What will you do in the solemn day, in the day of the festivity of our Lord?

text.NRSV: For even if they escape destruction, Egypt shall gather them, Memphis shall bury them. Nettles shall possess their precious things of silver; thorns shall be in their tents.
text.Rotherham: For, though they have gone from destruction, yet, Egypt, shall gather them, Memphis, shall bury them,—as for their silver favorites! thistles, shall possess them, thorns in their tents.
text.KJV: 9:6 For loe they are gone, because of destruction: Egypt shall gather them vp, Memphis shall burie them: the pleasant places for their siluer, netles shal possesse them: thornes shall be in their Tabernacles.
text.DRV: 9:6 For behold they are gone forth from destruction: Ægypt shall gather them together, Memphis shall bury them: their silver to be desired the nettle shall inherit, the bur in their tabernacles.

text.NRSV: The days of punishment have come, the days of recompense have come; Israel shall know it. The prophet is a fool, the man of the spirit is mad, because of your great iniquity and great enmity.
text.Rotherham: Come are the days of visitation, come are the days of recompense, Let Israel know! The prophet is foolish, the man of the spirit doth rave, because of the greatness of thine iniquity, therefore great is the prosecution.
text.KJV: 9:7 The dayes of visitation are come, the dayes of recompence are come, Israel shall know it; the Prophet is a foole, the spirituall man is madde, for the multitude of thine iniquitie and the great hatred.
text.DRV: 9:7 The days of visitation are come, the days are come of retributions: know o Israel the foolish Prophet, the mad spiritual man, for the multitude of thine iniquity, the multitude also madness.

text.NRSV: The prophet is a sentinel for Ephraim with my God, yet a fowler’s snare is on all his ways, and enmity in the house of his God.
text.Rotherham: The watchman of Ephraim, [should have been] with my God: As for the prophet, the snare of the fowler, is on all his ways, a prosecution [awaiteth him], in the house of his God.
text.KJV: 9:8 The watchman of Ephraim was with my God: but the Prophet is a snare of a fouler in all his wayes, and hatred in the house of his God.
text.DRV: 9:8 The watchman of Ephraim with my God: the Prophet is made a snare of ruin upon all his ways, madness in the house of his God.

text.NRSV: They have deeply corrupted themselves as in the days of Gibeah; he will remember their iniquity, he will punish their sins.
text.Rotherham: They have deeply corrupted themselves, like the days of Gibeah: he will call to mind their iniquity, he will punish their sins.
text.KJV: 9:9 They haue deeply corrupted themselues as in the dayes of Gibeah: therefore he will remember their iniquitie, he will visite their sinnes.
text.DRV: 9:9 They have sinned deeply, as in the days of Gabaa: he will remember their iniquity, and will visit their sin.

text.NRSV: Like grapes in the wilderness, I found Israel. Like the first fruit on the fig tree, in its first season, I saw your ancestors. But they came to Baal-peor, and consecrated themselves to a thing of shame, and became detestable like the thing they loved.
text.Rotherham: Like grapes in the desert, found I Israel, like the first-ripe in the fig-tree when it is young, saw I your fathers,—they, entered Baal-peor, and devoted themselves to the Shameful Thing, Then became their abominations like their lusts.
text.KJV: 9:10 I found Israel like grapes in the wildernesse: I saw your fathers as the first ripe in the fig tree at her first time: but they went to Baalpeor, and separated themselues vnto that shame, and their abominations were according as they loued.
text.DRV: 9:10 As grapes in the desert I found Israel: as the first fruits of the fig-tree in the top thereof I saw their fathers: but they have entered into Beelphegor, and are alienated into confusion, and are become abominable, as those things, which they loved.

text.NRSV: Ephraim’s glory shall fly away like a bird—no birth, no pregnancy, no conception!
text.Rotherham: As for Ephraim! like a bird, did their glory, fly away,—no birth, and none with child, no conception.
text.KJV: 9:11 As for Ephraim, their glory shall flee away like a bird: from the birth and from the wombe, and from the conception.
text.DRV: 9:11 Ephraim as a bird hath flown away, their glory from birth, and from the womb, and from conception.

text.NRSV: Even if they bring up children, I will bereave them until no one is left. Woe to them indeed when I depart from them!
text.Rotherham: Yea, though they rear their children, yet will I make them childless, till there be no human being,—for it is, nothing less than woe, to them when I depart from them!
text.KJV: 9:12 Though they bring vp their children, yet wil I bereaue them that there shall not be a man left: yea, woe also to them when I depart from them.
text.DRV: 9:12 But if they shall nourish up their children, I will make them without children among men: yea and woe to them, when I shall depart from them.

text.NRSV: Once I saw Ephraim as a young palm planted in a lovely meadow, but now Ephraim must lead out his children for slaughter.
text.Rotherham: Ephraim! just as I provided for Tyre, was planted in a meadow,—yet, Ephraim, must needs bring forth for a murderer his children.
text.KJV: 9:13 Ephraim, as I saw Tyrus, is planted in a pleasant place: but Ephraim shall bring foorth his children to the murderer.
text.DRV: 9:13 Ephraim, as I saw, was Tyre founded in beauty: & Ephraim shall lead out his children to the murderer.

text.NRSV: Give them, O Lord—what will you give? Give them a miscarrying womb and dry breasts.
text.Rotherham: Give them, O Yahweh—what wilt thou give? Give them, a miscarrying womb, and breasts dried up.
text.KJV: 9:14 Giue them, O Lord: what wilt thou giue? giue them a miscarying wombe, and drie breasts.
text.DRV: 9:14 Give them o Lord, what wilt thou give to them? Give them a womb without children, & dry breasts.

text.NRSV: Every evil of theirs began at Gilgal; there I began to hate them. Because of the wickedness of their deeds I will drive them out of my house. I will love them no more; all their officials are rebels.
text.Rotherham: All their wickedness, is in Gilgal, yea, there, have I come to hate them, For the wickedness of their doings—out of my house, will I drive them forth,—no more will I love them, all their rulers, are unruly.
text.KJV: 9:15 All their wickednesse is in Gilgal: for there I hated them: for the wickednesse of their doings I will driue them out of mine house, I will loue them no more: all their princes are reuolters.
text.DRV: 9:15 All their wicked in Galgal, because there I hated them: for the malice of their inventions I will cast them forth out of my house: I will not add to love them, all their princes revolters.
